summary: `Logger` methods are not called when debugger breaks on script error

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.5.dev [46c495ca21f40f57a7fb9c7cde6143738f1652d4]

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta (46c495ca2) - Fedora Linux 42 (Adams) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon Graphics (RADV GFX1201) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 60.39 GiB memory

### Issue description

The new `Logger` class that was introduced in #91006 is meant to be able to capture all errors and log messages for as long as its registered. However, if you're playing your game through the editor, and haven't enabled the "Ignore Error Breaks" toggle in the Debugger panel, any GDScript error (like accessing a null reference) will only trigger a debugger break but won't actually emit any error message for `Logger` to pick up on.

When looking at the code, the suppressing of these error messages seems to be deliberate:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/46c495ca21f40f57a7fb9c7cde6143738f1652d4/core/debugger/remote_debugger.cpp#L106-L109
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/46c495ca21f40f57a7fb9c7cde6143738f1652d4/core/debugger/remote_debugger.cpp#L412-L429

This might have made sense before the introduction of `Logger`, but seeing as how your `Logger` could be set up to forward errors to some error reporting service or wherever, I would argue that it becomes somewhat problematic for `RemoteDebugger` to be suppressing errors like this.

I would maybe go so far as to argue that the current behavior isn't necessarily intuitive either.

There's also another intertwined issue here, which is that the `ERR_PRINT(error_str)` seen above, which the "Ignore Error Breaks" toggle makes use of in order to let through the previously suppressed error, results in the location of the error being misreported, since it always reports `RemoteDebugger::debug` as the location rather than the script it actually originated from.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP.
2. Run the main scene.
3. Note how the debugger breaks at the null access.
4. Note how there's no error in the "Errors" tab in the "Debugger" panel.
5. Note how `MyLogger` hasn't printed anything.
6. Enable the "Ignore Error Breaks" toggle, under the "Stack Trace" tab in the "Debugger" panel.
7. Run the main scene again.
8. Note how the debugger doesn't break at the null access.
9. Note how there is an error in the "Errors" tab in the "Debugger" panel.
10. Note how `MyLogger` has printed an error.
11. Note how the location of the error is incorrect.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[logger-script-breaks.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20793157/logger-script-breaks.zip)

summary: Using duplicate variable names causes orphaned nodes without any error messages

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7628) - AMD Ryzen 9 7950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

- I discovered an orphan node appearing in my project. After printing it out, I found it was a TextureRect, yet I never generated or removed any TextureRect through code, nor did I find any error logs. Troubled by this, I conducted an investigation. Eventually, I identified the cause, with reproducible steps and examples provided below.  
- I'm uncertain whether other classes might exhibit similar behavior.  
- Ideally, such cases should either throw an error or automatically free themselves.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a GDScript inheriting BaseButton  
- Export a variable named `icon` of type TextureRect (it shares the same name as the `icon` in the Button class)  
- Attach it to a Button node  
- Create a TextureRect child node for the Button node and assign it to the exported `icon` variable  
- Run the project and discover that an orphan node is generated

[button_test_2025-06-18_11-59-52.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20788419/button_test_2025-06-18_11-59-52.zip)

summary: ColorPicker Node's popup features break if used inside of a child native window.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in:
- v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]
- v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Pop!_OS 22.04 LTS on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050 Ti (nvidia; 565.77) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-8300H CPU @ 2.30GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

ColorPicker Node 's  Popout menu functionality breaks while inside a Window with ``flags > force_native`` to true. All of its popout elements are displayed inside of the main Window instead of the window it was called on.

The things affected are  Picker shape, Loading Swatches, button Hints, and  Selecting a Picker type via the dropdown menu.
While the color sampler simply doesn't work in this context. (But I believe that's a separate bug)

# Screenshots

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4d53bff6-17d4-4218-a948-675cd7aa9c57)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d64a12db-4fb3-4915-9029-c1ea459e04fc)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e82725a4-c6a0-41d6-a436-a62f32a15f7c)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/db2255cb-09d6-4cf2-bbbe-3ee6d4c4a519)

# Disclamer:
**NOT TO BE CONFUSED With Bug #107647 , thats for ColorPickerButton, this is  ColorPicker node.  They have very similar results but different reproduction and limitations it might be caused by the same system (i found this one while working around #107647 in my project)** 

### Steps to reproduce

Add a Window node, then set ``flags > force_native``  and ``flags > wrap_controls``  to true in the inspector. (`wrap_controls` is optional but makes it better to see)

Hide the window node. (so that it opens as a native window later)

Add a Button Node.
On the Button Connect `pressed()` to the Window's `Show()`

Then add a ColorPicker Node as a child of the Window.

Run the Scene, click the button Node.
The windows opens up, then click the buttons for  Picker shape, Loading Swatches, Selecting a Picker, and button Hints (when you mouse over a button that textbox that apears)

You will quickly notice that they appear on the Main Window instead of the window you are using and If you try to interact with it should close, making impossible to use.

It should look like this:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b9641f27-0bd0-4f8c-a685-47a29ce50189)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Here's a Scene that reproduces the bug, just put it in the project, run it,  and do the last two steps of Steps to reproduce. And a wild bug appears.

[ColorPickerWindowPopUpBug.tscn.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20786125/ColorPickerWindowPopUpBug.tscn.txt) *note: Uploaded as a txt beacuse github doesnt like TSCN

summary: Implement `EditorRangeDial` on top of `EditorSpinSlider`

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e06c51f3-19ae-4be0-820c-c3a724eb25ad

This dial-like ruler allows users to select a number with greater precision or the opposite. Unlike the current version, which allows dragging in the horizontal direction only. - [ ] Add docs

summary: Navigation3D  stuck/jitter at going down slope because of incorrect point calculation.

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4.1 stable on windows 11.

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7652) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

Project is using navigation3d demo. (just for example, I found this bug in my own project too).
https://godotengine.org/asset-library/asset/2743
The bug represents as stuck/jitter, but inner cause is NavigationAgent keeps giving same point. So of course object won't move anywhere.
I didn't use the original code, but requested a re-calc path every frame (using same target_position).
`character.gd`
```gdscript
func _physics_process(delta):
	_nav_agent.target_position = _target_pos
	
	if _nav_agent.is_navigation_finished():
		return
	var next_position := _nav_agent.get_next_path_position()
	var offset := next_position - global_position
	global_position = global_position.move_toward(next_position, delta * character_speed)
	# Make the robot look at the direction we're traveling.
	# Clamp y to 0 so the robot only looks left and right, not up/down.
	offset.y = 0
	look_at(global_position + offset, Vector3.UP)
```



### Steps to reproduce

1.download https://godotengine.org/asset-library/asset/2743
2.open with godot 4.4.1
3.change character.gd to code above. (or set a position every frame in your own way)
4.run and see bug.

[navigation.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20778923/navigation.zip)

description.text:
### Tested versions


- Reproducible on 4.3.stable and 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7652) - AMD Ryzen 5 5500 (12 threads)

### Issue description

When using  `GPUParticles3D` node on `Compatibility` renderer and on exported HTML (Web) builds, if you set the `GPUParticles3D` > `Draw Order` = `View Depth` the web browser console will show WebGL errors shown:

performance warning: READ-usage buffer was read back without waiting on a fence. This caused a graphics pipeline stall.
`

performance warning: READ-usage buffer was written, then fenced, but written again before being read back. This discarded the shadow copy that was created to accelerate readback.
`

1. Using the Compatibility renderer create a Node3D root scene with:

- Directional Light
- Camera3D (position camera so you can see the GPU Particles)
- GPUParticles3D

4. Add a QuadMesh to Pass 1

6. Add HTML template for export
7. Run in browser from the editor (Shift+F5)

summary: Code complete on $-derived variables doesn't work past the 2nd statement

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproduced in:
- v4.4.1.stable.fedora [49a5bc7b6] (latest stable build)
- v4.5.dev5.official [64b09905c] (latest dev build)

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable (49a5bc7b6) - Fedora Linux 42 (Workstation Edition) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) HD Graphics 5500 (BDW GT2) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-5600U CPU @ 2.60GHz (4 threads)

### Issue description

The `$Selector` syntax is sugar for **Node.get_node()** with a quality-of-life bonus in the script editor: it is aware of the actual node class and thus offers a complete list of members is the picklist. However, when assigned to a variable, it works only on the immediate statement, and not past the 2nd one. Let me demonstrate.

Assume a simple scene with a single child node **Label**. I `$`-select the child and store it in a var.

✅ On the next line in my script, I get a complete picklist of the **Label** members, **get_line_count()** is one of them.

![complete-on-next-line.png](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/98c8de0c-6151-4423-b452-94a1fc11fb46)

❌ On lines not immediately followed by the var assignment, I get a picklist for **Node** class only. E.g. the aforementioned **Label**'s method **get_line_count()** is not offered anymore.

![complete-past-2nd-line.png](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/eb203f60-e16a-4343-8fd6-e9e05ae60bea)



### Steps to reproduce

1. Make a new, blank project.
2. Create a 2D scene (Node2D).
3. Add a child node, say Label.
4. Attach a script to the root node Node2D.
5. Try:
```
extends Node2D
func _ready() -> void:
	var lbl := $Label
	# Try code complete "lbl.getlc" on this line: get all Label members
	var _foo := 42
	# ...and then try code complete "lbl.getlc" on this line: get Node members only
```

summary: Make `PROPERTY_HINT_GROUP_ENABLE` hide properties by default

description.text:
- `PROPERTY_HINT_GROUP_ENABLE` was added in #105272, being checkbox-only.
- #105623 added the `feature` hint string to indicate that unchecking the checkbox should hide all properties in the group.

However, in the Godot repository, __all__ properties using this hint set the hint string to `feature`. This shows that being checkbox-only is not a good default.

Since this is a new feature in 4.5, let's change the default behavior before the API becomes stable.

summary: Allow fixing indirect missing dependencies manually

description.text:
Currently, the dependency error dialog is only shown for missing dependencies that are referenced directly by the opening scene. Therefore, if the missing dependency is deeply nested, manually fixing it becomes cumbersome. I guess this is the main reason we devote to improving automatic solutions. However, there are situations where manual fixing is inevitable.

This PR lists all missing items related to the current scene in the Dependency Errors dialog. It also allows the user to open the corresponding dependency editing dialog directly from this dialog, so that they don't have to manually locate them one by one in the file system.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c4e6f8d9-d28d-45da-81bf-5645ecf4e5fb

Note: Ideally, fixing one missing resource should ideally fix all references to it automatically. However, this would involve a complete refactoring of the dependency editing dialog. I think that could be a separate PR.

Test project with several missing dependencies: [deps-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20751796/deps-mrp.zip)

Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12681

summary: GPUParticles2D resize when `emitting` is enabled

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.5.dev.custom_build [1218a16de]

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev (1218a16de) - macOS Sequoia (15.5.0) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M4 Pro - Apple M4 Pro (14 threads) - 24.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

When setting the `emitting` property of a GPUParticles2D node to `true`, the size of existing particles appears to be re-calculated. This has a very jarring effect.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f8241c7b-1e67-435c-9569-6c8307764ce2

As I was preparing to file this report, I found https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/104511 which sounds like basically the same problem, except with the 3D particle system instead of the 2D one. A merged PR that sounds like it should've fixed the issue, including for GPUParticles2D, is linked there, but for me it seems the issue persists (or is a different one).

### Steps to reproduce

Create a GPUParticles2D system with a ParticleProcessMaterial where the display scale has a range. Disable `emitting`, then re-enable it; any particles that were already there will change their scale instantaneously.

summary: Implement ```use_shared_copy``` for Resource

description.text:
This PR only affects **internal resources**, external resources (saved to .res, tres, or in .tscn) will stay shared (if ```local_to_scene``` is false).

TL;DR After this PR you can do this in Godot by default:

![pr_vid_1](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d27221ab-c86f-4c61-aaf7-c9dbaca9b064)

# Related proposals:
godotengine/godot-proposals#4672
godotengine/godot-proposals#9603
godotengine/godot-proposals#11312

# Keynotes (from proposals)
* We cannot determine if a resource should use a full copy or shared copy solely by its type. (eg: Material)
* If forcing internal resources to use full copy every time, it might unintentionally cause bloat and performance issues. 
* Adding pop-ups every time a node is duplicated for users adjust the resource copy is not good for UX, and some people is against it.

# Conclusion (mine)
* The solution needs to provide users the option to determine if a full copy is needed **per resource**, not per class.
* The solution also has to provide **reasonable defaults** that fits most of the use cases, so user don't need to adjust resource copy behavior frequently.
* External resources should always stay shared after node duplication
* As in those proposals we didn't come to an common agreement, I'd say we can at least have a **simple solution, which works well for now, also being easy to refactor**, and keep discussing our "perfect solution" later.

# Solution
## Implement property ```use_shared_copy``` in ```Resource```
So I come up with this PR, acts as a new proposal and proof of concept:

![pr_img_1](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d632162b-7109-42d7-b2c7-1ebfdb2e0d4a)

Defaults to true (use shared copy) for most resources, and **defaults to false (use full copy) where reasonable**. If user wants a specific resource to behave differently on copy, they can adjust it freely.

This boolean is only taken into account at ```Node::duplicate()```, it doesn't affect ```Resource::duplicate()```. It's main purpose is to improve UX when using editor duplicate tool.

It also doesn't conflict with ```Resource::local_to_scene```. It only accounts for node duplication in the current local scene.

## Resources that defaults to full copy
Whether a resource should default to full copy, we need to consider the following (top priority to lowest):
1. If user **DOES NOT** notice it's shared, how likely will he lose work because of it?
2. If user **DOES** notice it's shared, will it take a lot of time by managing it?
3. If user **DOES NOT** touch the default behavior at all, will it possibly cause too much bloat?

If a developer cares much about optimization (point 3), they will likely discover this option actively by themselves. But most devs do not care and will not know the resource is shared or not unless they run into problems, worst of all, those problems are mostly related to lose of work and time (point 1), this could drive users away from Godot, it's serious.

Here's the list of resource classes that defaults to **full copy**, IMO:
```
// Internal resources that uses full copy by default

Animation
AnimationLibrary

BoxShape3D
CapsuleShape3D
CylinderShape3D
SphereShape3D
SeparationRayShape3D
WorldBoundaryShape3D

Shape2D (All derived classes)

CameraAttributes (All derived classes)

Curve
Curve2D
Curve3D

Environment

Noise (All derived classes)

Gradient

LabelSettings

MultiMesh

OccluderPolygon2D
```

Some resources have very even pros and cons when deciding to make it default to full-copy on node duplication, I'll write my decision down here and open for discussion:

* ```Material``` **(shared copy)**: Very likely to run into draw call performance hit if using full copy every time, and possibly time-consuming to manually set all nodes to use the same Material again.

* ```Animation```, ```AnimationLibrary``` **(full copy)**: The most possible scenario where this will be copied is duplicating a ```AnimationPlayer``` node, and the reason for copy is most likely -- **It's an animation created using Godot, the user wants a full copy of the animation and modify it**. Another common scenario is **The user wants to share animation to multiple similar skeletons**, which is a more advanced usage, I consider the user doing this is more likely to think of sharing resources, and even if they don't, it'll still work. We should make this default to full copy for novice users that only wants to animate something in Godot.

* ```Shape3D```, ```Shape2D``` **(depends on shape)**: Whether to use full copy by default depends on **how likely the shape is going to be tweaked by hand**, and performance costs. Namely ```ConcavePolygonShape3D```, ```ConvexPolygonShape3D```, ```HeightMapShape3D``` will default to use shared copy, the others uses full copy.

summary: Glow in Compatibility makes screen texture very dark
Deadlock when calling Activator.CreateInstance() to instantiate a node while ResourceLoader.LoadThreadedRequest() is running in the background

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4.1
Reproducible in 4.5 dev 5
Not reproducible in 4.3


### System information

Godot v4.5.dev5.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-9400F CPU @ 2.90GHz (6 threads)

### Issue description

(Note: the system information section says Windows 10, but I was also able to reproduce this on a Windows 11 machine)

If you call `Activator.CreateInstance()` to instantiate a node while a load started by `ResourceLoader.LoadThreadedRequest()` is running in the background, _and_ the timing happens to be just right, the game will permanently freeze.  Looping sounds will continue playing, but everything else is completely frozen.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the attached MRP
2. Run it
3. Click "Sunset Castle"---you'll be taken to a loading screen featuring a character doing a funny dance
4. If the bug is reproducible on your machine, the freeze will occur when the character's hips are swung to the right of the screen.  If "Sunset Castle" does not trigger the freeze for you, try "Broken Glass Factory" instead.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[FastDragon_MinimalFreeze.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20741003/FastDragon_MinimalFreeze.zip)

Sorry the MRP is so large; if I remove too many things from the scene being loaded, the bug becomes MUCH harder to consistently reproduce.  I suspect that's because a race condition is at play, and making the loaded scene smaller throws off the timing.

summary: PinJoint2D not working when process mode is disabled during instantiate, even if re-enabled after

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot version : 4.4.1

Custom build: No

Reproduced in official build: Yes

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+)

### Issue description

Hello,

I've encountered what seems to be a strange behavior when working with RigidBody2D and PinJoint2D.

When linking two RigidBody2D nodes using a PinJoint2D, the joint does not properly constrain the bodies if process_mode is temporarily set to PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED, even if it is immediately restored to PROCESS_MODE_INHERIT within the same function.

Expected behavior:
After restoring process_mode to INHERIT, the PinJoint2D should behave normally and apply constraints to both bodies.

Actual behavior:
The PinJoint2D appears in the scene, but it has no effect — the two RigidBody2D nodes act as if they are not connected at all.

This might be intended behavior, but it doesn’t seem to be.

### Steps to reproduce

Create two RigidBody2D spheres.

Temporarily set root node process_mode = PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED.

Immediately create a PinJoint2D connecting them.

Set root node process_mode back to PROCESS_MODE_INHERIT.

Run the scene and trigger the behavior (e.g., right-click in provided example).

Here is the test project :

[PinJointFail.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20739194/PinJointFail.zip)

Left click → joint works

Right click → joint fails (when process_mode is disabled and restored)

summary: VisualShader inconsistent behavior across many node types for entering values in input fields

description.text:
### Tested versions

-Reproducible in: 4.4.1 Stable

### System information

Linux Mint 22.1, Godot 4.4.1 Stable

### Issue description

**The issue:**

Inside the visualshader editor, for any node with values , when clicking any field to input another value, one the following happens depending on the type of node:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8d391a6d-d045-4fcb-a646-150af2790fec)
**a) Node with single input field:** The value is automatically selected and allows easy and fast editing.

**b) Node with single input field:** The value opens a secondary popup to edit the value, the popup is focused but does not select the value, requires additional Enter key press or mouse click to be editable.

**c) Node with multiple input fields for Vector3** The value is selected and can be edited, pressing Tab to select the next value only works if first value is the same as current value, but after editing to a different value Tab key confirms and does not navigate to the next field (when available), if no value is edited Tab ends navigation after the last field.

**d) Node with single input field for Vector3:** The value opens a secondary popup, the popup is focused but does not select the value, requires additional Enter key press or mouse click to be editable, Tab key correctly switch focus to the next field, keeps looping focus after the last field.

So, nodes that use the secondary popup do not automatically select the value for easy editing, or nodes that don't have said popup missing the Tab focus navigation, making the workflow inconsistent and requiring so many more additional key presses slowing down the whole process.

**How I expected it to work?**

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1494368c-e3bc-4bc1-b895-46c6d564697e)
Basically the Vector3Constant node is the expected behavior.
Click the value, the pertinent value is automatically selected for fast and easy editing, Tab key navigates to the next field, looping focus after last field may not be required depending on who you ask.

See above

## Summary
Adds a toggle button in the Animation Track Editor that allows users to choose between inserting animation keys and markers at the timeline cursor position or at the mouse position.

## Changes
- Add `editors/animation/insert_at_timeline_cursor` editor setting to persist toggle state
- Add toggle button UI in Animation Track Editor toolbar  
- Modify key/marker insertion logic to use cursor position when toggle is enabled
- Add documentation for the new editor setting
- Icon by @TokageItLab

Fixes #103272

## Demo

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/770923d0-2976-4769-8126-3945efcd8d63

- Supersedes/closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/105774

summary: Hexagonal Tiles Rotating 90° instead of 60°, Making them misaligned

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Found in v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6] (haven't checked in other versions).

### System information

Windows 11 - Godot  v4.4.1.stable.mono.official

### Issue description

When you try to turn an hexagonal tile (using rotate left or rotate right) in a TileMapLayer and place it manually, it is rotated 90° instead of 60, making it look misaligned

## Example here : 

![Example](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bbca7a00-5f3a-474e-87da-25657c6ca227)

# From the start :
- Create Project
- Create a TileMapLayer as a child of a Node2D
- Add a tileset (hexagonal)
- Go to TileSet on the bottom and add an atlas with a least one tile in it
- Go to tileMap, select a tile, rotate it once (left or right) and try to place it on the editor
You should have reproduced the bug.

- Select one of the tiles of the Tilemap, rotate it once and try to place it.

summary: Simplify `TranslationDomain` Workflow

description.text:
This PR introduces the ability to configure `translation_domains` in the project settings and assign a `translation_domain` per Node.

This is useful in scenarios where, for example, a player may prefer different languages for subtitles and voice-over. It simplifies the localization workflow for developers.

You can define translation domains in the Project Settings:
![Bildschirmfoto_2025-06-13_19-53-29](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df00eb8d-ee3a-4ab9-9267-2c82c0e934aa)
And assign a custom translation domain per Node:
![Bildschirmfoto_2025-06-13_19-53-57](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3924c7fe-3b8e-47d2-91bb-52abd07f415f)
All child nodes will inherit the same translation domain, unless explicitly overridden.

For instance, if you want to use English voice-over with German subtitles, you can set the main language to English and override the subtitles domain to use German.

The changes are split across multiple commits for easier review.

Closes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11610
Closes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/6254
